tagline: Clean tech for the bottom line
problem: Climate tech solutions exist but are fragmented, expensive to deploy, and don't integrate with existing industrial infrastructure.
solution: An integrated climate platform that makes sustainability measurable, manageable, and monetizable.

insights.title: Regulatory Tailwind
insights.description: EU CSRD and SEC climate disclosure rules are creating mandatory reporting for 50,000+ companies. Your market just got bigger by law.

insights.title: Data Network Effects
insights.description: Every customer improves your emissions data model. Build a data consortium to create switching costs.

insights.title: Budget Constraints
insights.description: Sustainability budgets are often the first cut during downturns. Prove hard dollar savings, not just ESG impact.

insights.title: Partnership Strategy
insights.description: Partner with carbon credit marketplaces and renewable energy providers to offer an sustainability stack.

website.summary: Mission-driven website with strong visual storytelling, but needs more technical depth on data methodology.
improvements: Add integration directory for ERP systems
recommendations: Lead with ROI — show how sustainability saves money
Add a compliance calendar showing reporting deadlines by region
Publish a carbon accounting methodology guide
Include a self-service emissions estimator tool

brand.mission: To make sustainability the default operating system for every business.

logos.description: Leaf encased in a circular refresh arrow — sustainability cycle
Abstract globe with growing sprout — planet-first design
Three overlapping leaves forming a gear — nature meets industry

icp.title: Chief Sustainability Officer
icp.role: Sustainability executive at a mid-to-large enterprise
icp.description: Environmentally conscious consumer looking to reduce their carbon footprint through better technology.
painPoints: Carbon accounting is still done in spreadsheets
ESG reporting frameworks are inconsistent across jurisdictions
Supply chain emissions data is nearly impossible to collect
Green technology ROI is unclear to CFOs
goals: Achieve ESG reporting compliance in EU and US
Get supply chain suppliers to report emissions
objections: "Sustainability is a cost center, not a revenue driver"
"We already have consultants doing our ESG reporting"
"We don't have budget for another SaaS tool"
recommendations: Create an ESG compliance checklist tailored to the user's industry
Publish case studies showing quantifiable ROI from sustainability
Build pre-built integrations with SAP, Oracle, and Workday

revenue.model: Pre-seed B2B SaaS — initial pricing set at $500/mo/mo
revenue.justification: At pre-seed, pricing should incentivize early adoption. Consider a 'design partner' discount in exchange for product feedback and case study rights.
revenue.analysis: Climate Tech Carbon — At pre-seed, pricing should incentivize early adoption. Consider a 'design partner' discount in exchange for product feedback and case study rights.

items.title: Data integration API
items.description: Connect to utility providers and ERP systems

items.title: Reporting dashboard
items.description: Generate ESG reports in major frameworks

items.title: Iterate based on usage data
items.description: Ship 3 feature iterations based on user feedback, measure NPS > 40

items.title: Achieve first revenue
items.description: Convert 10% of free users to paid, reach $2K MRR

items.title: Scale acquisition channels
items.description: Identify 2 channels with CAC < LTV/3, spend $5K/mo profitably

items.title: Hire first employee
items.description: Bring on a full-stack engineer or sales lead to accelerate growth

verdict.summary: Climate Tech Carbon: Your startup scored 59/100. timing is your strongest dimension (90/100). distribution needs the most attention (35/100).

market.label: Rapidly Expanding
market.description: Climate tech funding reached $15B+ in 2024. Regulatory tailwinds from CSRD and SEC rules create mandatory spending.

timing.label: Urgent Timing
timing.description: Regulatory deadlines (SEC, CSRD) create mandatory spending. Net-zero commitments drive urgency.

competition.label: Fragmented Competition
competition.description: Climate tech is still emerging. Watershed, Persefoni exist but no clear winner. Regulatory changes shift landscape.

defensibility.label: Data Network Effects
defensibility.description: More customers = better emissions data models. Regulatory compliance creates retention.

founderFit.label: Developing
founderFit.description: Your founder-market fit is developing. Deeper domain expertise will strengthen your position.

distribution.label: Regulatory-Led Sales
distribution.description: ESG compliance creates buyer urgency. CFOs control budgets, not CSOs.

revenue.label: Compliance-Driven
revenue.description: ESG reporting is mandatory for public companies. Regulatory mandates create inelastic demand.

strengths.explanation: You're building at the right moment. Market conditions favor your entry.
Your market is large, growing, and has strong tailwinds.

weaknesses.explanation: Customer acquisition is a critical risk. Rethink your distribution strategy.
Competition exists but there's room for differentiation.

improvementPaths.action: Ship MVP and get first 10 design partners
improvementPaths.risk: Delay launch by 6+ months for perfection

improvementPaths.action: Narrow from broad market to a specific vertical
improvementPaths.risk: Expand too early into adjacent verticals

improvementPaths.action: Work in the industry you're disrupting for 3 months
improvementPaths.risk: Hire a CEO who doesn't understand the problem

improvementPaths.action: File provisional patents or build proprietary data assets
Identify and amplify your unfair advantage
improvementPaths.risk: A well-funded competitor enters your space
Rely entirely on outbound enterprise sales

roast.verdict: Climate Tech Carbon — Climate tech has the tailwinds but faces the longest sales cycles of any vertical. Your buyers have budgets but no authority, and authority figures have no budgets.
risks: ESG reporting is mandatory for public companies but discretionary for private
Carbon credit markets are immature and reputationally risky
Your own operations may have significant carbon footprint
Pre-seed startups have 6-12 months of runway on average
Founder-led sales doesn't scale — you need repeatable motions
recommendations: Target regulated industries first (EU, California) where reporting is mandatory
Publish your own carbon footprint transparently
Integrate with existing ERP systems rather than replacing them

items.feedback: CSOs have influence but rarely budget authority. You need to also sell to CFOs who control P&L.
Carbon data is inherently estimated. Be transparent about your methodology and margin of error.
Watershed, Persefoni, and Salesforce Net Zero Cloud all exist. What's your unfair advantage?
You cover EU CSRD but what about SEC climate rules and California SB 253? Coverage breadth is a competitive moat.
Manual data entry kills adoption. You need automated ingestion from utility APIs and ERP systems.
ESG budgets are small. Consider per-report pricing rather than annual subscriptions to lower entry barriers.
